# Assignment Polling Contract

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/assignmentcontract -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

> Riido task: RIID-4687

Executable SSOT: [`assignment/assignment_contract.riido.json`](../../assignment/assignment_contract.riido.json).

This is the public generated reader for the C10 assignment polling contract shared by riido-daemon and riido-control-plane.

- Evidence artifact: `assignment-contract-evidence`
- Contract schema: `riido-ai-server-contract.v1`
- Service schema: `riido-ai-server.v1`
- States / poll actions / task events: `8 / 4 / 14`

## Responsibility

- `assignment` owns the shared `riido-ai-server.v1` DTO contract, assignment states, legal transitions, poll actions, task events, approval values, execution identity, payload snapshot fields, and runtime binding DTOs.
- Markdown readers must link to `assignment/assignment_contract.riido.json` instead of redefining the matrix by hand.
- The contract fixture is decoded as one strict JSON document; unknown fields and trailing JSON are failures.

## Boundary

- This package does not own control-plane store actors, HTTP handlers, SSE fan-out, request authorization, provider process execution, Terraform, DynamoDB/EventBridge adapters, AWS account settings, secret values, or deployment evidence.
- `Health` and `MetricsSnapshot` remain in `riido-control-plane` until they are promoted from adapter/read-model contracts.
- Private repositories remain private/unsupported until a future token-reference broker exists; raw tokens, signed URLs, and credentialed clone URLs must not enter assignment payloads, prompts, events, logs, RAG context, or daemon clone arguments.

## Assignment States

| State | Agent active | Terminal | Transitions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `queued` | `false` | `false` | leased, cancelling, cancelled, failed |
| `leased` | `true` | `false` | ready, running, cancelling, cancelled, failed |
| `ready` | `true` | `false` | running, cancelling, cancelled, failed |
| `running` | `true` | `false` | completed, failed, cancelling, cancelled |
| `cancelling` | `false` | `false` | cancelled, failed |
| `cancelled` | `false` | `true` |  |
| `completed` | `false` | `true` |  |
| `failed` | `false` | `true` |  |

## Poll Actions

| Name | Value |
| --- | --- |
| `None` | `none` |
| `Start` | `start` |
| `Cancel` | `cancel` |
| `Active` | `active` |

## Assignment Payload Fields

| Field | Source | Snapshot | Required | Consumer |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `agent_instruction` | `agent.instruction` | `assignment-created` | `false` | riido-daemon provider-specific runtime instruction placement |
| `allow_experimental_runtime` | `agent.runtime.requires_experimental_opt_in` | `assignment-created` | `false` | riido-daemon runtime scheduling experimental opt-in gate |
| `model_id` | `agent.model_id` | `assignment-created` | `false` | riido-daemon provider runtime model selection |

## Invariants

- `Assignment.allow_experimental_runtime` is assignment-created snapshot data derived by control-plane and consumed by daemon; daemon must not infer it from provider name or local environment.
- `Assignment.model_id` is assignment-created snapshot data for runtime model selection; daemon must not re-resolve model defaults from provider name, environment, team id, or Open API key configuration.
- While an assignment is `leased`, `ready`, or `running`, the daemon sends an assignment heartbeat every 5 seconds.
- The control plane treats the active assignment lease as stale when it has not been refreshed for 20 seconds, and late heartbeats/events must not revive it.
- `PollRequest.wait_ms` is an optional long-poll hint. Omitted or zero preserves the legacy short-poll wire shape.
- `PollResponse.action=active` must include a self-contained assignment snapshot so a restarted daemon can recover local in-flight state without starting fresh.

## Evidence Loop

| Step | Evidence |
| --- | --- |
| Observe | Assignment polling had a strict executable contract and package tests, but the public reader was hand-maintained prose and the workflow did not publish JSON evidence. |
| Hypothesis | Generating the reader and evidence from the assignment contract manifest will keep state/action/payload governance aligned across daemon and control-plane while preserving dependency-map anchors. |
| Execute | Strictly decode the assignment contract, render the generated reader, check reader drift, and publish assignment-contract evidence in public CI. |
| Evaluate | The verifier fails on malformed manifest, stale generated reader, missing workflow evidence binding, malformed contract JSON, unknown fields, or missing strict artifact upload. |
| Retrospective | Assignment polling governance becomes a contract-backed loop instead of prose maintained from memory. |
